standard_description: Tell and write time to the nearest minute and measure time intervals in minutes. Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of time intervals in minutes, e.g., by representing the problem on a number line diagram.
subject: Math

problems.text:
Problem 1
Look at your yardstick and 12-inch ruler again.
Ms. Cole says that 1 foot is equivalent to
$$\frac{1}{3}$$
yard. Do you agree or disagree? Explain.
Using similar reasoning, determine what fraction of a foot an inch is.
Use your reasoning from Part (a) to solve the following conversions.
$$\frac{2}{3}$$
yard = ______ feet
$$2\frac{1}{3}$$
yards = ______ feet
$$\frac{5}{12}$$
foot = ______ inches
$$3\frac{1}{4}$$
feet = ______ inches
$$4\frac{1}{2}$$
yards = ______ inches
Problem 2
a.  Show or explain how you know
$${{1\over16}}$$
pound =
$$1$$
ounce.
b.  Show or explain how you know
$${1\over4}$$
pound =
$$4$$
ounces.
c.  Show or explain how you know
$$4{3\over8}$$
pounds =
$${67}$$
ounces.
Problem 3
Solve.
a.
$${{1\over4}}$$
gallon = ________ quart
b.
$${{3\over2}}$$
quarts = ________ pints
c.
$${4{1\over2}}$$
pints = ________ cups
d.
$${7{3\over4}}$$
cups = ________ fluid ounces
Problem 4
Solve.
a.
$${{1\over24}}$$
day = ________ hour
b.
$${{13\over60}}$$
hour = ________ minutes
c.
$${2{1\over4}}$$
minutes = ________ seconds
d.
$${3{1\over2}}$$
days = ________ minutes

Problem 1
The clock below shows what time Dante starts cooking dinner. What time does Dante start cooking?
###IMAGE0###
Problem 2
Dante is done cooking at 6:21 p.m. Draw hands on the clock below to show when Dante is done cooking.
###IMAGE1###

Task
It usually takes Dajuana 45 minutes to do her homework. If she starts her homework at 5:30 PM, what time will she finish?
One day Dajuana started her homework at 6:45 PM and finished her homework at 7:20 PM. How long did Dajuana spend on her homework?
Another day, Dajuana finished her homework at 5:05 PM after spending 40 minutes on her homework. What time did Dajuana start her homework?

Problem 1
a.   Henry starts riding his bike at 3:10 p.m. He rides for 35 minutes. What time does he stop riding his bike?
b.   Alfonso starts exercising at 7:20 a.m. He finishes exercising at 7:45 a.m. How many minutes did Alfonso exercise?
c.   Cassie goes swimming for 30 minutes. She finishes swimming at 1:50 p.m. What time did she start swimming?
Problem 2
a.   Joe finishes his chores at 5:42 p.m. It took him 28 minutes to complete them. What time did he start doing his chores?
b.   Lucia’s math class started at 10:18 a.m. She worked for 33 minutes. What time was it when Lucia’s math class ended?
c.   Leslie starts reading at 11:27 a.m. She finishes reading at 11:54 a.m. How many minutes does she read?

Problem 1
Ms. Glynn was so hungry! She kept looking at her watch to see how much time there was until lunch. Write down the time she checked her watch underneath each clock below:
###IMAGE0###
Problem 2
Determine the times displayed on the following clocks.
a.
###IMAGE1###
b.
###IMAGE2###
c.
###IMAGE3###
d.
###IMAGE4###
Problem 3
Draw hands on the clock below to display the time written in each part.
a.   2:05
###IMAGE5###
b.   5:45
###IMAGE6###
c.   8:28
###IMAGE7###
d.   11:56
###IMAGE8###
Before a movie starts, a theater plays some movie trailers. The trailers start at 8:05 p.m. and end at 8:24 p.m. How long are the movie trailers?

Problem 1
Determine the time to the minute before the hour. Phrase your answer as “___ minutes to ___.”
a.     7:48
b.     5:32
Problem 2
Determine the time. Write it in standard form.
a.   Four minutes until 5
b.   26 minutes until midnight

Problem 1
a. How many seconds are in a minute?
b. How many minutes are in an hour?
c. How many hours are in a day?
d. How are each of these relationships represented on an analog clock? A picture of one is shown below.
###IMAGE0###
Problem 2
Fill in the following conversion tables.
###IMAGE1###
Problem 3
Anne, Matt, Cara, and Ross ran a half marathon but had staggered starts, so they aren’t sure who had the best time. They each recorded their run times in the following way:
###TABLE0###
Problem 4
United Airlines has the following contest:
###IMAGE2###
You have the following flight information:
###TABLE1###
What would you submit as your contest entry?

Problem 1
Farida’s recess starts at the time displayed on the clock below. It ends at 1:45 p.m.
###IMAGE0###
Plot points on the number line below that represent what time Farida’s recess starts and ends. Then explain how you decided where to plot them.
###IMAGE1###
Problem 2
Plot the following times on the number line below.
7:15 a.m.
7:55 a.m.
7:23 a.m.
7:39 a.m.
###IMAGE2###
Problem 3
Plot the following times on the same number line.
3:48 p.m.
3:37 p.m.
4:01 p.m.
4:14 p.m.

Problem 1
The third-grade teachers left school on Friday to go to a movie together. It's currently 2:12 p.m. and the movie’s start time is 3:00 p.m. Here is what Google Maps told them they should do to get to the movie theater:
###IMAGE0###
Will they make it in time to see the movie? Show or explain your work.
Problem 2
a.   Joey gets home at 3:25 p.m. It takes him 7 minutes to unpack and 18 minutes to have a snack before starting his homework. What is the earliest time Joey can start his homework?
b.   Shane’s family wants to start eating dinner at 5:45 p.m. It takes Shane 15 minutes to set the table and 7 minutes to help put the food out. What time should Shane start his chores so that he’ll be ready to eat at 5:45 p.m.?
c.   Davis has 3 problems for math homework. He starts at 4:08 p.m. The first problem takes him 5 minutes, and the second takes him 6 minutes. If Davis finishes at 4:23 p.m., how long does it take him to solve the last problem?

Problem 1
Solve.
a.   7 days = ____________ hours
b.   12 minutes 34 seconds = ____________ seconds
Problem 2
Jacob needs to do his chores. It takes Jacob an hour and 45 minutes to mow the lawn and 20 minutes to clean his room. If he starts his chores at 2:00 PM, what time will he finish?

Problem 1
Mr. Wynn was enjoying teaching the fourth graders and he lost track of time. When he looked at the clock, pictured below, he exclaimed, “Oh my gosh, it’s already 5 to 3!” Angelica says, “That’s not what time it is. It’s 2:55.” Who is correct, Mr. Wynn or Angelica? Explain how you know.
###IMAGE0###
Problem 2
Determine the time.
a.   10 minutes before 4
b.   Quarter to noon
Problem 3
Determine the time to the minute before the hour.
a.   9:52
b.   5:43

Problem 1
Act 1: Look at the following image -
###IMAGE0###
a.   What do you notice? What do you wonder?
b.   How much juice is left?
Problem 2
Act 2: Use the following information to solve -
###IMAGE1###
Someone has already drunk four servings of juice, each of which are 10 fluid ounces.
Problem 3
Act 3: Reveal the answer –
How Much Apple Juice - Act 3
Was your answer reasonable? Why or why not?
Problem 4
Act 4 (the sequel):
How many more full servings of juice are left in the bottle?

Problem 1
Melissa woke up this morning at the time shown on the clock below.
###IMAGE0###
Colton wakes up 25 minutes later than Melissa. Gemma wakes up 18 minutes later than Colton. What time does Gemma wake up?
Problem 2
Ayesha spends 17 minutes on her math homework and 14 minutes on her history homework. She spends 25 minutes reading. How many more minutes does Ayesha spend on her math and history homework than she does reading? Show or explain your work.

Problem 1
Itoro practiced piano for
$$1\frac{1}{4}$$
hours. Tiffani practiced piano for twice as long as Itoro. For how many minutes did Tiffani practice piano?
Problem 2
Mary and her friends are making fruit punch. They combine 1.5 liters of lemonade, 3 liters of pineapple juice, and 0.25 liters of cranberry juice. How many total milliliters of fruit punch did they make?

Plot points on the following number line that correspond with each time below. Label each point with its corresponding letter a–d. Be as precise as possible.
###IMAGE0###
5:45 p.m.
###IMAGE1###
6:12 p.m.
###IMAGE2###

Problem 1
a.   It usually takes Dajuana 45 minutes to do her homework. If she starts her homework at 5:30 p.m., what time will she finish?
b.   On the weekend, Dajuana started her homework at 11:45 a.m. and finished her homework at 12:20 p.m. How long did Dajuana spend on her homework?
c.   Another day, Dajuana finished her homework at 5:05 p.m. after spending 40 minutes on her homework. What time did Dajuana start her homework?
Problem 2
a.   Ms. Banta leaves school at 4:52 p.m. She gets home at 5:13 p.m. How long was Ms. Banta’s commute home?
b.   Katherine wakes up from a nap at 2:26 p.m. Her watch tells her that she slept for 43 minutes. What time did Katherine fall asleep?
c.   On New Year’s Eve, Fernando’s parents wake him up so that he can watch the ball drop. He wakes up at 11:48 p.m. He’s up for 19 minutes before going back to bed. What time did Fernando go back to bed?

Problem 1
Watch the following video:
The Water Boy (Act-1)
.
###TABLE0###
How much water did he drink?
Problem 2
Watch the following video:
Act 1: On a Roll
.
This is how much tape she has:
###IMAGE0###
The length of the dry erase board is
$$1\frac{5}{12}$$
feet and the width of the dry erase board is
$$1\frac{11}{12}$$
feet.
Will she have enough tape to cover the border of the dry erase board?

Minna’s soccer practice ends at the time shown on the clock below. Practice was 55 minutes long. At what time did Minna’s soccer practice start?
###IMAGE0###
Elysia, Ivan, and Silvio each go on a run. Elysia ran 1 mile. Elysia ran four times as far as Ivan. Silvio ran 5 fewer yards than Ivan. How far, in feet, did Silvio run?

Problem 1
Show or explain how you know
$${2{3\over4}}$$
gallons =
$${11}$$
quarts.
Problem 2
Solve.
a.
$${1{2\over3}}$$
yd = _____ ft
b.
$${3{3\over8}}$$
lb = _____ oz
c.
$${4{5\over6}}$$
ft = _____ in
d.
$${1{1\over2}}$$
hr = _____ min

tasks.task_text:
3.MD Dajuana's Homework
Task
a. It usually takes Dajuana 45 minutes to do her homework. If she starts her homework
at 5:30 PM, what time will she finish?
b. One day Dajuana started her homework at 6:45 PM and finished her homework at
7:20 PM. How long did Dajuana spend on her homework?
c. Another day, Dajuana finished her homework at 5:05 PM after spending 40 minutes
on her homework. What time did Dajuana start her homework?
3.MD Dajuana's Homework

khan_exercises.title: Telling time on a number line
khan_exercises.description: Tell time to the nearest 5 minutes using a number line.
topic_path: Module 2: Place value and problem solving with units of measure
Topic A: Time measurement and problem solving
Telling time on a number line

items.question_content:
Look at the following number line.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4e5f5f8bb712a70e662b731f56f0107955cf8dd2)

**What time is shown on the number line?**

[[☃ numeric-input 3]] $:$ [[☃ numeric-input 4]]
hints: The $\goldE{A}$ is located after the hour of $8$ o'clock but before $9$ o'clock.  So the hour is $8$.

Each tick mark on the number line represents one minute.

The $\goldE{A}$ is located $5$ tick marks after $0$ minutes. So the minutes are $5$.
The time is $8{:}{05}$.

items.question_content:
Look at the following number line.

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/39fd89579ac78afe666501182feda90549270f5a)

hints: The $\goldE{A}$ is located after the hour of $7$ o'clock but before $8$ o'clock.  So the hour is $7$.

Each tick mark on the number line represents one minute.

The $\goldE{A}$ is located $10$ tick marks after $0$ minutes. So the minutes are $10$.
The time is $7{:}{10}$.

The time is $10{:}40$.

items.question_content:
The number line below represents one hour on a clock. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0d4231d3be9afcd564988d174d6878b603a16309)

**Where is $2{:}05$ graphed on the number line?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Each tick mark on the number line represents one minute.

$2{:}05$ is $5$ minutes after $2{:}00$.  

So, we move $5$ tick marks past $2{:}00$. 

![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6efec6ed0099e9a3d79df2d73818911d155d5847)
$2{:}05$ is graphed at $\goldE{A}$ on the number line.

choices.content: $\goldE{A}$
$\blueE{B}$
$\maroonD{C}$

items.question_content:
**On which number line is $10{:}55$ graphed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Each tick mark on the number line represents one minute.

$10{:}55$ is $55$ minutes after $10{:}00$. 

![The number line goes from 0 to 60 with marks at every 10 minutes. 0 is labeled 10:00 and 60 is labeled 11:00. Marks divide the space between each number into 10 equal parts. The mark at 55 is labeled 10:55.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dc6b31a5fc3964b73362cfec505346cc126bb49a) 

So, we move $55$ tick marks past $10{:}00$.

This number line shows $10{:}55$ graphed:

![The number line goes from 0 to 60 with marks at every 10 minutes. 0 is labeled 10:00 and 60 is labeled 11:00. Marks divide the space between each number into 10 equal parts. Point A is labeled at the fifth mark to the right of 50.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/480bf5ecce2d7b9448f851ccecd8a47372aec56d)

choices.content: ![The number line goes from 0 to 60 with marks at every 10 minutes. 0 is labeled 10:00 and 60 is labeled 11:00. Marks divide the space between each number into 10 equal parts. Point A is labeled at the fifth mark to the right of 50.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/480bf5ecce2d7b9448f851ccecd8a47372aec56d)

![The number line goes from 0 to 60 with marks at every 10 minutes. 0 is labeled 9:00 and 60 is labeled 10:00. Marks divide the space between each number into 10 equal parts. Point B is labeled at the fifth mark to the right of 50.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e829e93c4c7da4128008f6a50b5abbda9d843b9c)

![The number line goes from 0 to 60 with marks at every 10 minutes. 0 is labeled 10:00 and 60 is labeled 11:00. Marks divide the space between each number into 10 equal parts. Point C is labeled at the mark on 50.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7626fe0a19905a541e7c4b29dc531e7c9a9330ee)

items.question_content:
**On which number line is $1{:}05$ graphed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Each tick mark on the number line represents one minute.

$1{:}05$ is $5$ minutes after $1{:}00$. 

![The number line goes from 0 to 60 with marks at every 10 minutes. 0 is labeled 1:00 and 60 is labeled 2:00. Marks divide the space between each number into 10 equal parts. The label 1:05 is at the fifth mark to the right of 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c5ee37509b7bd22991888f171b48147b42fef737) 

So, we move $5$ tick marks past $1{:}00$.

This number line shows $1{:}05$ graphed:

![The number line goes from 0 to 60 with marks at every 10 minutes. 0 is labeled 1:00 and 60 is labeled 2:00. Marks divide the space between each number into 10 equal parts. Point A is labeled at the fifth mark to the right of 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8a874e834d22c533d61f3c13ebb045e4fd4398fd)

choices.content: ![The number line goes from 0 to 60 with marks at every 10 minutes. 0 is labeled 1:00 and 60 is labeled 2:00. Marks divide the space between each number into 10 equal parts. Point A is labeled at the fifth mark to the right of 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8a874e834d22c533d61f3c13ebb045e4fd4398fd)

![The number line goes from 0 to 60 with marks at every 10 minutes. 0 is labeled 5:00 and 60 is labeled 6:00. Marks divide the space between each number into 10 equal parts. Point B is labeled at the 10 mark to the right of 0.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/12f3341d2440763fe4f235af7840d6c1f578d19e)

![The number line goes from 0 to 60 with marks at every 10 minutes. 0 is labeled 1:00 and 60 is labeled 2:00. Marks divide the space between each number into 10 equal parts. Point C is labeled at the fifth mark to the right of 10.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3b08a98f268d8f4d09047125e32bcefa90d7c870)

items.question_content:
**On which number line is $2{:}40$ graphed?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Each tick mark on the number line represents one minute.

$2{:}40$ is $40$ minutes after $2{:}00$. 

![The number line goes from 0 to 60 with marks at every 10 minutes. 0 is labeled 2:00 and 60 is labeled 3:00. Marks divide the space between each number into 10 equal parts. The label 2:40 is at point 40.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/46f00c948609e1c7a79214023845b680cde78127) 

So, we move $40$ tick marks past $2{:}00$.
